transcript.text: Hey, living in Boulder, V-Log for 4th of July, 2019. It's 5.02 p.m. on Thursday.
Yesterday, I did a video maybe in the afternoon, and I was working on buildings on Atlantis on Google Earth.
And, yeah, that's still what I'm doing.
Today, or yesterday, I, you know, I had to do the ground and adjust the ground,
And then that meant I had to readjust the heights of the buildings, which I built first and didn't realize.
And so I had to redo the heights of all my pyramids, which was a long time because it was 15 layers per pyramid.
And just all these different things, and even adding some buildings, you know, filling up all the gaps and stuff.
And so that's just still what I'm doing now.
I'm doing Tower of London, and then I have to do some other building.
And, yeah, and, like, fill in a couple more blocks there, and I think everything's accounted for at that point inside the city.
I want to make perches, too, you know, with the movie tool.
And maybe even some movies, I don't know.
But, yeah, and then I'll get going.
Maybe today I can get going on the outside of the city.
I'll definitely do it tomorrow.
And, yeah, hopefully that won't go on for more than a couple days.
I definitely would like to be done before Tuesday.
That would be one month, four weeks.
So, yeah, maybe just a couple more days.
And then I can get back to that project that I wanted to do before, which was Google Earthing all the kingdoms of the world.
You know, if I could knock those suckers out real fast.
So, yeah, I'm just trying to finish Atlantis here.
But the way that Atlantis works out is really cool.
You know, like, you get the prescribed, you know, roads that come in from the outside, like spokes of a wheel.
I chose them based on what Google Earth made when I made the circle.
You know, they had it themselves.
And then I added one extra road once I got in to where the houses are, like the final five or whatever.
And those blocks are perfectly sized for a track, like a running track and, you know, like a Beverly Hills house lot and lots of stuff.
So, yeah, it works out really symmetrically.
Yeah, listening.
I was listening to Plato Dialogues today.
Oh, but today I went to hang out with my mom, you know.
I hung out with her yesterday.
I was helping her out with stuff.
And then somebody had the idea to ride downtown.
Ride downtown.
She's a fat 77-year-old lady who can't ride a bike, but she has a tricycle.
So, she rode her tricycle.
We went down up the river, the bike path up the river to Legal Pizza and had a beer.
And then rode back on the other bike path.
And, yeah, that was pleasant.
Just about what she could do.
That was a couple hours out of my day.
I was going to help do gardening, but I didn't do that.
But she did make me a salad in the beginning and pay for my beer.
Oh, yeah, and half of a burrito.
Yeah, so now I'm getting going.
I haven't even really started that much yet.
And, yeah, I'll just work all day, all night and finish it today.
And then, yeah, I'll have it ready for download in maybe a week or something.
Yeah, I haven't decided what to do with the outside of the city, but there's a few things I have to do, you know.
There's 60,000 lots, 1.85 kilometers by 1.85 kilometers, which is big.
And there's an 1,850 kilometer long trench that's 185 meters wide that I have to wind around.
And every 18.5 kilometer, another canal came off the side of it.
And I think I'm going to build it off of this trench that's inside of the Bahamas.
It's perfectly straight and three miles across.
Looks totally false, not natural to me.
I'm interested to see what somebody else has to say about that.
So that's what I'm up to.
I had a beer, and then I had another beer at my mom's house, and then a shot of whiskey.
She has alcohol over there right now.
So, and then I had to smoke some more weed now.
So, relax to get going.
So, that's it.
Hopefully tomorrow when I do the video, it's possible I'll be done with the actual city.
That would be amazing if I am.
And then actually working on the country itself.
That'd be cool.
That'd be progress.
So hopefully that's what will be going on.
All right, talk to you tomorrow.
Bye-bye.
